target.title: fix(memory): add batch completed log after embedding batch finishes
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `batch completed` and `batch failed` debug logs to the embedding batch retry loop so operators can distinguish hung batches from completed ones without external tracing.

- Problem: `embedBatchWithRetry` logged "batch start" but had no corresponding completion or failure log, making it impossible to tell from logs alone whether a batch was still running or had finished.
- Solution: Add `log.debug("memory embeddings: batch completed", ...)` after `runEmbeddingOperationWithTimeout` resolves successfully, and `log.debug("memory embeddings: batch failed", ...)` in the catch handler.
- What changed: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-ops.ts` — +10/-1 lines (3 log statements)
- What did NOT change: Embedding provider behavior, retry logic, timeout handling

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra — diagnostic observability improvement

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #93312
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression — diagnostic improvement only, does not fix the underlying hang

## Motivation

When debugging embedding batch hangs (#93312), the "batch start" log was the only indicator of activity. Without a corresponding completion or failure log, operators had no way to know whether a batch completed, failed, or was still running. This PR adds those missing log lines.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: The embedding batch retry loop now emits "batch completed" after each successful batch and "batch failed" for each failed batch attempt.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v24.13.1, commit `4421872`, OpenClaw v2026.6.9

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

The proof uses the existing OpenClaw test infrastructure to create a real `MemoryIndexManager` with a mock embedding provider, index a test memory file, and capture the `log.debug()` output from the actual `embedBatchWithRetry` code path:

```bash
OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/memory-core/src/memory/proof94732-v3.test.ts --run
```

- **Evidence after fix — REAL RUNTIME DEBUG LOG OUTPUT from embedded `.sync()` path**:

```
[memory] sync: indexing memory files
[memory] embeddings: batch start
[memory] embeddings: batch completed
```

Both `batch start` (pre-existing) and `batch completed` (added by this PR) appear in the real runtime output. The `batch failed` log is structurally identical and sits in the same method's catch handler.

Full integration test proof:

```console
$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/memory-core/src/memory/proof94732-v3.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose

 RUN  v4.1.8

 ✓ |extension-memory| proof94732: embedBatchWithRetry debug log emission > emits embedding batch debug logs during sync (864ms)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)

=== Captured debug log output ===
  [memory] embeddings: batch start
  [memory] embeddings: batch completed
```

The test assertions verify:
- `embeddings: batch start` is present in the real runtime output
- `embeddings: batch completed` is present in the real runtime output

This proves the `embedBatchWithRetry` code path is correctly exercised end-to-end and produces both debug logs at runtime.

Successful completion log format produced by the PR's code:
```
[memory] embeddings: batch completed { provider: "mock", items: <count> }
```

Failure log format (catch handler, structurally identical):
```
[memory] embeddings: batch failed { provider: "mock", error: "<formatted error>" }
```

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. Source code contains "batch completed" debug log after successful batch completion
  2. Source code contains "batch failed" debug log in the catch handler
  3. Error field uses `formatErrorMessage(err)` for provider secret redaction — no bare `String(err)` in error fields
  4. Real runtime execution confirms both `embeddings: batch start` and `embeddings: batch completed` are emitted during a `MemoryIndexManager.sync()` operation
  5. Log format includes provider ID and batch item count / error message for diagnostics

- **What was not tested**: Live embedding endpoint with real HTTP roundtrip (requires a running openai-compatible embedding provider). The underlying batch hang issue (#93312) is not addressed by this PR.

## Root Cause

N/A — this is a diagnostic observability improvement. The prior code had no completion/failure signal for individual embedding batches.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts`, `manager-embedding-timeout.test.ts`, `manager-embedding-policy.test.ts`
- 37 existing tests continue to pass unchanged (the PR only adds log statements, no behavior changes)

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. The new logs are at `debug` level and only visible when `OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug` is set. No functional behavior change.

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Real `MemoryIndexManager.sync()` execution emits `embeddings: batch completed` log via the actual `embedBatchWithRetry` code path
- Edge cases checked: Log format consistency (provider ID, item count, error message); `formatErrorMessage` usage for secret redaction
- What you did NOT verify: Live embedding endpoint with real HTTP traffic

## AI Assistance 🤖 (required for AI-assisted PRs)

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-flash
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest-risk area**: None — this is a log-only change with no behavioral impact.
- **Mitigation**: The log statements are at `debug` level, not visible in normal operation.
- **Compatibility impact**: None.

---

Related to #93312
target.author: xydt-tanshanshan
